      "それはトルグオという名のスラブ人の集落があります。",
      "それは10世紀に建てられた石造りの城があります。",
      "それは1119年に市場があったことが証明されています。",
      "それはエルベ川を渡る渡し場があります。",
      "それはハーテンフェルス城があります。",
      "それは1543年から1544年にかけて建設された宮廷礼拝堂があります。",
      "それはフランシスコ会修道院があります。",
      "それは醸造所博物館があります。",
      "それはロシアとアメリカ軍の会合の記念碑があります。",
      "それはロシア軍人墓地があります。"

    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはノルﾄﾞザクセン郡の首都であることを特徴としています。",
      "それはエルベ川のほとりにあることを特徴としています。",
      "それは第二次世界大戦におけるアメリカ軍とソ連軍の最初の会合の場所であることを特徴としています。",
      "それはドイツで最も完全に保存されている初期ルネサンス様式の城があることを特徴としています。",

      "それはアメリカ陸軍第一軍とソ連第一ウクライナ戦線の会合の場所であることを特徴としています。",

      "それはドイツ、ザクセン州北西部にあるエルベ川のほとりの町です。",

      "それは世界最古のおもちゃ屋、カール・レープナー・トルガウで知られています。"

About: It is about the meeting of the United States and Soviet Armies near the end of World War II.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Slavonic settlement named Turguo.
It is associated with the shire of Neletici.
It is associated with the Holy Roman Empire.
It is associated with the duchy of Saxe-Wittenberg.
It is associated with the Electorate of Saxony.
It is associated with the Ascanian dynasty.
It is associated with the Wettin dynasty.
It is associated with the Ernestine line of the Wettin dynasty.
It is associated with the Albertine line of the Wettin dynasty.
It is associated with the Reformation.
BelongsTo: It belongs to northwestern Saxony, Germany.
BuiltIn: It was built in the 10th century.
ComposedBy: It was composed by Luther, Melanchthon, Bugenhagen and Jonas.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Torgau proper and several municipal divisions.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 10th century.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 10th century.
HasA: It has a Slavonic settlement named Turguo.
It has a stone castle built in the 10th century.
It has a market attested in 1119.
It has a ford crossing the river Elbe.
It has Hartenfels Castle.
It has a court chapel constructed in 1543-44.
It has a Franciscan monastery.
It has a brewery museum.
It has a monument for the meeting of Russian and American troops.
It has a Russian military cemetery.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being the capital of the district Nordsachsen.
It is characterized by its location on the banks of the Elbe.
It is characterized by being the site of the first meeting between United States and Soviet Armies in World War II.
It is characterized by having the largest completely preserved castle of the early Renaissance in Germany.
It is characterized by being the location where the Torgauer Artikel was composed.
It is characterized by being the location where the Lutheran Formula of Concord was written.
It is characterized by presenting the first German opera.
It is characterized by being the location of the Battle of Torgau.
It is characterized by being the location of the meeting of U.S. First Army and the Soviet First Ukrainian Front.
It is characterized by having Special Camps Nos. 8 and 10 established by the NKVD.
HeldIn: It was held in the shire of Neletici.
It was held in the Holy Roman Empire.

InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Treaty of Leipzig partition of the Wettin inheritance.

It was influenced by the Yalta Agreement.
IsA: It is a town on the banks of the Elbe in northwestern Saxony, Germany.
It is the capital of the district Nordsachsen.
It is a settlement that goes back to a Slavonic settlement named Turguo.
It is a town located on the important trade route, the Via Regia Lusatiae inferioris.
It is a town that belonged to the duchy of Saxe-Wittenberg.
It is a town that fell to the Ernestine line after the Treaty of Leipzig.
It is a town that fell to the Albertine line after the Battle of Mühlberg.
It is a town where the town council closed all cloisters in 1523.
It is a town where Katharina von Bora died and is buried.
It is a town where the first German opera was presented.
KnownAs: It is known as the place where the United States and Soviet Armies first met near the end of World War II.
KnownFor: It is known for Hartenfels Castle, the largest completely preserved castle of the early Renaissance in Germany.
It is known for its court chapel, consecrated by Luther on 5 October 1544, the second oldest newly constructed Protestant church in the world.
It is known for the Torgauer Artikel, a draft of the Augsburg Confession.
It is known for the Lutheran Formula of Concord.
It is known for the Battle of Torgau, a major battle of the Seven Years' War.
It is known for the meeting of the United States Army and the Soviet Army on 25 April 1945, remembered as Elbe Day.
It is known for the film Encounter at the Elbe.
It is known for the Memorial Site Torgau, researching the history of the Torgau prisons.
It is known for being the world's oldest toy store, Carl Loebner Torgau.
